But then there are several tests in TestDateTimeZone that were previously passing but now failed after the mutation. These include testForOffsetHours_int and others, which suggests that changing how the offset is formatted might have introduced bugs in these areas.

So, I need to determine if the mutant code change (line 291) is adjacent to any other changes that could be causing issues. The output format requires a boolean indicating whether this mutation is fault-adjacent. Fault-adjacent usually means that it's near other changes or parts of the code that might have caused the issue.

In this case, since only one line was changed in offsetFormatter(), and it directly affects how time zones are formatted, I think that mutant_id should be "src-main-java-org-joda-time-DateTimeZone-291-3". As for is_fault_adjacent, since other tests are failing because of this change, it's likely that the mutation is causing issues elsewhere. Therefore, I'll set is_fault_adjacent to true.
